item.text: I’ve narrowed the implementation down to a contained refactor inside the users domain: typed error and transaction sentinels, projection helpers in `models.go`, and narrower service-side repository contracts with nil-safe accessors instead of concrete assumptions. I’m applying those edits now before I run the scoped user-domain tests.

The dispatcher concluded without spawning specialists and confirmed the same implementation direction I’m already on. I’ve finished the main service-contract refactor and I’m checking for compile breaks in the touched users files before I adjust the scoped tests and run them.
The users package now compiles structurally after `gofmt`. I’m running only the scoped domain tests next, with a local Go cache so verification doesn’t depend on ambient runner state.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc 'python3 /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py append-decision --report-dir /workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/PR581-022 --agent-name dispatcher_agent --decision "Refactor users domain around narrower contracts and typed sentinels" --reason "The users package had several service methods coupled directly to the full repository contract and concrete projection helpers. item.text:
task_id: `PR581-022`  
status: `completed`

Changed the scoped users-domain files to make the package more substitutable without changing its external service API. The main changes were typed `UserError` sentinels in [errors.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/errors.go), shared projection/personal-link helpers in [models.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/models.go), narrower repository-facing contracts plus nil-safe normalization in [service.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/service.go), typed transaction constants in [transactions.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/transactions.go), and fake/test cleanup in [service_profile_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/service_profile_test.go) and [service_transactions_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/service_transactions_test.go).

files_changed: `backend/internal/domain/users/errors.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/models.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/service.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/transactions.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/service_profile_test.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/service_transactions_test.go`

checks_run: `GOCACHE=/workspace/socialpredict/.gocache GOMODCACHE=/workspace/socialpredict/.gomodcache go test ./internal/domain/users/...`  
checks_run: `GOCACHE=/workspace/socialpredict/.gocache GOMODCACHE=/workspace/socialpredict/.gomodcache go test ./handlers/users/...`

follow_ups: `./internal/domain/users/...` passed. `./handlers/users/...` still has unrelated failures already present in that package: `TestEmojiValidation/Text_with_HTML` and a `ListUserMarkets` path that hits a missing `bets` table in sqlite test setup.

Assumption: I treated the LSP alignment request as preserving existing public method signatures while reducing concrete coupling inside the users domain and normalizing fake/service behavior around narrower contracts and empty collection semantics.
